Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.

Cut the pita bread into 8 triangle shaped wedges. Lay the triangles out in a single layer
on the prepared tray and lightly spray with oil spray.

Bake for 5 minutes or until they begin to colour. Turn over the wedges and bake for
another 5-8 minutes until both sides are lightly coloured. Remove from the oven and allow to
cool.

Heat a small non-stick fry pan over medium heat. Add the balsamic vinegar, honey and
chopped pear and cook for 5 minutes or until the pear is lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Remove from the heat and set aside to cool.

To poach the egg: Fill a large saucepan with water until 8cm deep. Add the vinegar and
bring to the boil over a medium heat before reducing heat to a simmer.
Break the egg into the water and cook for 2-3 minutes for a semi-soft yolk or 3-4 minutes for a firm yolk. Remove the egg from the water using a slotted spoon.

Place the lentils, rocket leaves, spring onion, chilli and pear in a medium bowl. Drizzle over
some lemon juice and toss gently to combine.

To serve, place the lentil salad in a serving bowl and top with the goat's cheese and
poached egg. Serve with the pita wedges on the side. Enjoy!

Method
1 serve = 22 Molds (4.5cm)
Ingredients
For the batter
For the Mold coating
Steps

Pierre Hermé recommends that before you use your copper canele molds for the first time, clean them, and then season them by coating them with butter and placing them for 20 minutes in a hot oven - 250° C (500° F) or more. Remove them from the oven, clean them with a paper towel while they’re still hot, and allow them to cool before using. Never wash the molds, simply wipe with a dry cloth or a paper towel after each use.

Slit the vanilla beans lengthwise, and scrape the seeds from them with a small knife. Put the seeds, vanilla pods, and milk in a saucepan and bring them to a gentle simmer, and then turn off the heat and let sit for two minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk sugar, eggs and egg yolks. Add melted butter and whisk more until combined.

Remove the pods from the milk. Add 1/4 of the hot milk into egg mixture and mix well. Add flour and continue mixing until combined. Then slowly add the remaining milk while continuing to mix until all ingredients are mixed well. Add rum and mix. This way you will avoid lumps and will end up with the perfect consistency of the batter. (Note: this step is very important. Adding hot milk to egg mixture will temper the eggs and create custard which will ensure perfect consistency of your caneles).

Refrigerate for 24 to 48 hours. This is non-negotiable. The texture and flavor improves dramatically by the second day and third day. So, 24 hour rest is mandatory. 48 hours if you want amazing canele. The batter may be kept in a fridge for up to 4 days. Each time you use the batter, stir it well and work it at least 2 minutes with a whisk.

For a smooth, shiny surface that accentuates the dark brown color of the caneles coat the molds with a mix of 40 g beeswax and 60 g butter melted together. For this, melt the beeswax and butter together, quickly pour the mix into a mold and then back out into the original container. Turn upside down on a cooling rack with paper towel underneath to catch drippings. Once cooled down, chill in a freezer or a refrigerator until ready to bake.

Preheat oven to 280C. Fill the molds with batter to 3/8 inch (1 cm) from the top. Place on a foil lined backing sheet and bake the caneles at 280C for 10 minutes. Without opening the oven, drop the temperature to 190C and continue baking for another 45-50 minutes. Every oven is different, so your baking times may need to be adjusted.

Remove caneles from the oven. Quickly remove caneles from the molds and place on a cooling rack upside down. Caneles usually slide right out of their molds, but if they don't, turn the mold upside down and knock on a hard surface, such as a cutting board.
Let cool for 2 hours at room temperature. Caneles must be eaten on the day they are baked.
Notes

60 g bees wax and 90 g butter is enough to coat 20 2-inch canele molds, with just a little bit left over. You could probably do 22-24 molds with that amount. This time around, I heated canele molds in the oven and left them outside too long while I was shaving bees wax and then microwaving it with the butter. When pouring hot wax/butter mixture the molds were just warm to touch. I microwaved the shaved wax and the diced butter together on the highest power for 1 minute and 45 seconds, until I noticed it started to boil. Working quickly, I coated all 20 molds with a nice, even layer. Surprisingly, the temperature was just perfect to give a nice layer of wax. Before, I would sometimes get too thin a layer, because the molds were pre-heated too hot. So, heat your molds until warm to touch, heat wax/butter until first signs of boiling. Work quickly to pour the hot mix into molds all the way to the top, pour out, then turn over onto a rack to remove excess wax. For the sake of efficiency, I pour the wax into the next mold as I go, not back to the container, and top up with the mix from the container as needed.

For the soup
Steps

Heat a large pot or dutch oven over medium heat with the olive oil. When the oil is shimmering, add the carrots, celery, onion. Cook, stirring often, until the onions are translucent and the vegetables are tender; about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, salt, pepper, turmeric, rosemary and thyme. Cook for another 1 minute, stirring, until the garlic is fragrant.
Pour in the broth, increase the heat to medium-high and bring to a boil.
When the soup reaches a boil, reduce to a simmer and chopped chicken. Stir to combine and let cook, simmering uncovered and stirring occasionally, about 15-20 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice. Taste the soup and adjust seasonings, to taste. Serve and enjoy!
Method
To hard boil the egg, place the egg in a small saucepan and fill with cold water until it is covered by 2cm.
Bring the water to the boil over a high heat. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 5-6 minutes.
Tap the egg gently on the bench to crack the shell and peel. Slice into rounds.
Remove the egg from the saucepan with a slotted spoon and place in a bowl of iced water. Leave for a minute.
To serve, top the crispbreads with the spinach, egg and bean sprouts.
Enjoy!

For the
Steps
Curry Cashews (optional)

In a small bowl combine the oil, spices, salt and pepper. Add in the cashews and stir them until coated in the spice mixture. Add the cashew to a skillet heated over mediumlow heat. Let them toast for 35 minutes stirring them occasionally until they're golden brown and fragrant. Remove from the heat and set aside.
Creamy Red Lentil Soup

Heat the olive oil in a large pot over mediumhigh heat. When the oil is hot add in the onion, celery, and carrots. Season with salt and pepper. Sauté for 34 minutes. Add in the garlic, tomato paste, sriracha, and spices and sauté another minute.

Add in the red lentils and cilantro and pour in the broth and water. Stir everything together and bring the soup to a boil. Once boiling lower the heat to mediumlow and cover with a lid. Simmer for 1520 minutes or until the lentils are tender and starting to break apart.

Turn off the heat and use an immersion stick blender to purée the soup to your desired consistency. Pour in the coconut milk and lemon juice and stir until combined. Taste for seasoning and add more salt and pepper as needed. Garnish the soup with the curry cashews, cilantro, and extra sriracha if desired.

instructions.text: Ingredients
For the
For the
Steps
In a medium bowl, combine tomatoes, cucumbers, capers, and 2 Tbsp. olive oil. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, prepare LemonDill Yogurt Sauce:Combine yogurt, lemon juice, dill, garlic and 1 to 2 Tbsp. extravirgin olive oil; whisk to combine. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.

Heat 2 Tbsp. olive oil in a skillet over mediumhigh. Once hot, add eggplant and cook for 6 to 8 minutes, until nicely seared. Add tamari and cook 2 more minutes. Transfer eggplant to a bowl.Add remaining 2 Tbsp. oil to pan. Arrange halloumi slices in pan and cook until deeply golden, about 2 minutes per side. Watch closely to avoid burning or melting.Divide grain of choice (or arugula) evenly into four bowls. Top with sautéed eggplant, halloumi cheese, and a few spoonfuls of tomatocucumber salad. Add a big dollop of LemonDill Yogurt Sauce, and garnish with extra fresh dill and cracked black pepper.
Notes
MakeAhead: The lemondill sauce can be prepared up to 3 days ahead. Refrigerate in an airtight jar or container. You can also prepare the tomatocucumber salad up to 1 day ahead.
Store: Store grains, eggplant, and halloumi separately from salad and sauce.
Reheat: Reheat grain, eggplant, and halloumi mixture in the microwave or in a skillet over medium heat. Add tomatocucumber salad and sauce after bowl is warm.

Steps

Stir the tomato into the onions and garlic, then add the seasonings.
Stir everything together, then pour in the vegetable stock and the butter beans. Allow everything to simmer for a further 510 minutes on low heat until the sauce has thickened.

Ingredients
For the
For the
Steps
Adobo sauce: This is the sauce that chipotle peppers are canned in. It’s super smoky with a medium level of spice.
You can purchase a jar of pickled red onion or make them yourself (they’re super simple!). I follow this recipe.

Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss butternut squash with 2 Tbsp. olive oil, chili powder, smoked paprika, cumin, and 1/2 tsp. salt. Arrange in a single layer on a rimmed baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes.Remove from oven and add poblano pepper slices and a touch more oil; toss to incorporate with spices. Place pan back in the oven for another 15 to 20 minutes, or until squash is tender.

Meanwhile, prepare Avocado Sauce by combining cilantro, avocado, orange juice, oil, lime juice, and salt in a small blender or food processor (or use an immersion blender); blend until smooth. (You may need to add a splash of water or extra oil to thin out the sauce for more efficient blending.)

Drain black beans (no need to rinse). Shake off excess liquid and transfer to a microwavesafe bowl. Stir in adobo sauce and remaining 1/4 tsp. salt. Microwave for 45 to 60 seconds, until warm. Add to pan of roasted vegetables and toss well to incorporate with spices.

To assemble tacos, add a generous dollop of avocado sauce to each tortilla, and top with roasted butternut squash mixture. Garnish with pickled red onion, extra cilantro, cheese, and remaining half of avocado (sliced), if desired.
Notes
You can purchase a jar of pickled red onion or make them yourself (they’re super simple!). I follow this recipe.
To Store. Refrigerate taco filling in an airtight storage container for up to 4 days. Store leftover sauce separately for up to 1 week.

To Reheat. Rewarm the veggie filling in a large skillet on the stovetop over medium heat or gently in the microwave. Add avocado sauce after fillings are reheated. I also suggest warming your tortillas fresh each time.
To Freeze. Freeze taco filling in an airtight freezersafe storage container for up to 3 months. Let thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.

poblano pepper seeded and cut into strips, sub of choice
adobo sauce, from a can of chipotle in adobo sauce

Curatam ceapa si usturoiul si le tocam marunt. Curatam morcovii si ii taiem in felii subtiri. Curatam cartofii si ii taiem in cubulete potrivite.

Punem uleiul intr-o cratita, pe foc mic. Cand uleiul s-a incalzit, adaugam ceapa, usturoiul, morcovii si amestecam bine. Le calim pana cand ceapa se inmoaie, apoi adaugam cubuletele de cartof. Amestecam pe foc mic inca 1-2 minute si adaugam 100 ml apa usor calduta.
Cand morcovul si cartofii sunt fierti pe jumatate, adaugam mazarea (eu am folosit mazare congelata si am adaugat-o congelata in mancare).
Fierbem mancarea, pe foc mediu spre mic, inca cca 10 -15 minute, apoi adaugam apa ramasa, amestecata cu sucul de rosii si amidonul si amestecam bine.

Condimentam mancarea de mazare cu sare si piper si o lasam sa fiarba inca 5-6 minute sau pana cand sosul capata o consistenta usor cremoasa. Adaugam marar proaspat tocat, amestecam bine, stingem focul si acoperim mancarea de mazare cu un capac.
O servim calda, ca atare, sau ca garnitura la fripturi, snitele etc.

Steps

Melt the chocolate over a water bath or in your microwave on low power. Meanwhile, roughly chop

Method
Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Place the pumpkin on the lined tray and spray lightly with oil spray. Season with salt and pepper, if desired. Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until lightly browned and tender.
Heat the stock in a medium saucepan over medium heat.

Meanwhile, heat a large saucepan over medium heat and spray lightly with oil spray.
Add the mushrooms and sauté for 8 minutes or until tender and lightly browned, stirring occasionally. Remove from the heat and set aside in a bowl.

Return the saucepan to medium heat and spray lightly with oil spray. Add the leek and garlic and sauté for 4 minutes or until the leek is soft, stirring occasionally. Add the rice and thyme and cook for 2-3 minutes or until lightly toasted and fragrant, stirring frequently.
Pour one-quarter of the warmed stock into the pan and cook for 6-8 minutes or until most of the stock has been absorbed, stirring constantly

Add the remaining stock a ladleful at a time and allow the liquid to be absorbed before adding the next ladleful, stirring frequently. Cook for 20-25 minutes or until the stock has been used and the rice is cooked but still al dente. If all the stock is used and the rice is not ready, add 60ml of water at a time until cooked.
Gently stir in the pumpkin, lentils and spinach and cook until the pumpkin and lentils are heated through and the spinach has wilted, stirring frequently.

simmer, covered, for 5-6 minutes. Remove the egg from the saucepan with a slotted spoon
and place in a bowl of iced water. Leave for a minute. Tap the egg gently on the bench to
crack the shell and peel. Slice into rounds.

salt and pepper, if desired.

For the
Steps
Spălați ficățeii în apă rece ca să eliminați resturile de sânge din țesuturi. Scurgeți și uscați ficățeii tamponându-i cu prosoape de hârtie care să absoarbă excesul de apă.
Tocați mărunt ceapa și 2-3 căței de usturoi.
Puneți uleiul la încins în tigaie, apoi adăugați ceapa și usturoiul. Lăsați-le să se călească până când ceapa se înmoaie și devine ușor translucidă.
Puneți în tigaie ficățeii și condimentele, adică: piperul, cimbrul și frunza de dafin. Nu și sarea!
Lăsați ficățeii să se gătească timp de 10 minute amestecând din când în când. Își vor lăsa suficientă apă, nu e nevoie să mai adăugați.
Turnați roșiile pasate, amestecați și mai lăsați ficățeii pe foc încă 7-8 minute.
Stingeți focul, dați tigaia deoparte și puneți încă 2-3 căței de usturoi tocați mărunt și pătrunjel proaspăt.
Tot acum puneți și sarea. Amestecați și ficățeii sunt gata de servit cât sunt calzi.
Ficățeii cu sos de roșii se pot servi alături de un piure de cartofi, cartofi natur, orez sau mămăliguță.
Method

Heat the oil in a large non-stick fry pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 3-4 minutes or until soft and translucent, stirring occasionally. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring frequently. Add the paprika and turmeric and cook for a further 1 minute, stirring frequently.
Add the rice, capsicum, tomatoes and half of the stock to the pan. Simmer for 15 minutes or until almost all the liquid has been absorbed, stirring occasionally.

Add the peas, corn, chickpeas and remaining stock and cook for a further 5-8 minutes until the stock has been absorbed and the rice is tender, stirring occasionally. If the stock is all used and the rice is not ready, just add 60ml of hot water at a time until the rice is cooked.
Stir through the parsley and season with salt and pepper, if desired.
To serve, place the paella in a serving bowl. Serve with lemon wedges on the side.
Enjoy!

Ingredients
Steps
Preparare Spaghetti Alla Puttanesca

Daca folositi rosii proaspete, trebuie sa le opariti 30 de secunde in apa clocotita apoi sa le decojiti si sa le taiati in cubulete. Caperele sarate se limpezesc in cateva ape reci, ca sa mai piarda din sare. In rest, ingredientele sunt simple si cred ca se gasesc in orice camara.
Se pune pe foc o oala mare (5 litri) cu apa.

Usturoiul se zdrobeste. Maslinele si caperele se maruntesc, la fel si fileurile de ansoa si ardeiul chili. Pe acesta il folositi numai daca doriti, dar e recomandat. Eu am folosit ardeiul iute pentru ca imi place ca pastele sa aiba o nota picanta si pentru ca acesta e specificul acestor spaghete.

Se infierbanta uleiul de masline intr-o tigaie mare. Tineti cont ca in aceeasi tigaie trebuie sa incapa toate pastele fierte, la finalul prepararii. Se adauga usturoiul, maslinele, fileurile de ansoa si caperele. Se calesc 2-3 minute pe foc mediu. Se adauga rosiile cuburi (sau rosiile maruntite cu tot cu bulion, in cazul in care folositi conserva). Se fierbe sosul pe foc mediu, timp de 10-15 minute. Gustati de sarat si piperat si potriviti gustul sosului, dar de obicei nu prea mai e nevoie de sare, caperele, maslinele si fileurile de ansoa fiind sarate.

Cand apa din oala clocoteste, se pune o lingura plina cu sare si se adauga pastele. Cititi instructiunile de pe ambalajul spaghetelor, daca scrie ca timpul de gatire recomandat este de 9 minute, dupa exact 7 minute din momentul in care s-au pus la fiert se scot cu un cleste si se plonjeaza in tigaia cu sosul fierbinte. Se adauga cam 150-200 ml. din apa in care au fiert pastele in tigaie si se mai gatesc spaghetele in sos timp de doua minute, saltandu-le (sau intorcandu-le cu un cleste sau cu doua furculite) asa incat sa se imbrace bine in sos.

La final, sosul trebuie sa fie in paste, nu sa balteasca dizgratios pe langa acestea. Jumatate din patrunjelul tocat fin se adauga peste paste in tigaie, jumatate se pastreaza pentru presarat. Se monteaza spaghetele in farfurii, se presara cu patrunjel si… pofta buna!
Alte rețete delicioase de spaghetti:
Rețeta de spaghetti con le polpettine (cu chifteluțe)
Rețeta de spaghete cu fructe de mare

Cook, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage is slightly softened, about 5 minutes.

Add the broth, tomatoes, and bay leaves. Bring to a simmer; cover, and cook until the cabbage is tender, about 15 minutes. Stir in the white beans and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes. Remove the bay leaves.
Serve in soup bowls topped with parsley.
Method

In a small saucepan, bring the olive oil and water to the boil. Add the couscous and remove from the heat. Leave to stand, covered, for 2-3 minutes before "fluffing" with a fork to help separate the grains.
Cut a whole capsicum in half lengthways and clean out the seeds. Put one half aside to use at a later date.

Place the couscous, lentils, zucchini, pine nuts, orange zest and juice, parsley, half the goat's
cheese, salt and pepper in a mixing bowl and mix until well combined.
Fill the capsicum half with the couscous mixture and sprinkle over the remaining cheese. Place on the prepared tray and bake in the oven for 30-35 minutes or until the capsicum is tender.

To serve, place the stuffed capsicum on a serving plate. Serve with the spinach leaves on the
side.
Enjoy!
Method
Heat the oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes or until soft and translucent, stirring occasionally.

Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin and coriander and cook for 1 minute or until fragrant, stirring
constantly.
Add the rice, mushrooms, carrot and capsicum and cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly.

Add the stock and bring to the boil over high heat. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Remove from the heat and stir in the lentils. Leave to stand, covered, for 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork to separate the grains.
Season with salt and pepper, if desired.
To serve, place the pilaf in a serving bowl and sprinkle over the parsley.
Enjoy!

To serve, place the pad thai on a serving plate and sprinkle over the peanuts and coriander. Serve with lime wedges on the side.
Enjoy!

recipes.name: Quinoa & Roasted Beetroot Salad with Fennel & Goat's Cheese

instructions.text: Method
Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced).

Wrap the beetroot in foil with 1 tbsp of water (this helps the beetroot to steam). Place in a small roasting pan and bake for 30-40 minutes or until tender. Test the beetroot with a skewer - it is cooked if a skewer pierces the flesh easily. Set aside to cool. When cool enough to handle, peel and slice the beetroot. To save time, the beetroot can be cooked the night before and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

To cook the quinoa, bring the quinoa and 200ml water to the boil in a small saucepan over high heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10-12 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the quinoa is tender. To save time, the quinoa can be cooked the night before and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

Meanwhile, peel the orange over a medium bowl to catch the juice. Slice the orange or cut into
segments. Squeeze the remaining orange membrane to extract any extra juice into the bowl and set aside.
Place the beetroot, quinoa, fennel, cannellini beans, orange segments, rocket and lemon juice in a mixing bowl and toss gently to combine.

To serve, place the beetroot and quinoa salad in a serving bowl and top with the goat's
cheese.
Enioy!
Method
To cook the vermicelli noodles, place the noodles in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Leave for 10 minutes, then loosen the noodles with a fork. Drain and refresh under cold water. Drain well.

Meanwhile, whisk the garlic, soy sauce, lime juice and chilli flakes (if using) together in a shallow bowl. Add the tofu and gently turn to coat. Cover with plastic film and refrigerate for 30 minutes to marinate.

Heat a non-stick fry pan over medium heat and spray lightly with oil spray. Add the tofu and cook for 4-5 minutes or until all sides are browned, turning occasionally. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
Place the noodles, capsicum, snow peas, carrot, spring onion, spinach, coriander and mint in a mixing bowl and toss gently to combine.
To serve, place the salad in a serving bowl and top with the tofu. Serve with lime wedges on the side.
Enjoy!
Method

Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 5-6 minutes. Remove the egg from the saucepan with a slotted spoon and place in a bowl of iced water.
To serve, place the rye crispbreads on a serving plate and top with the sliced cucumber, egg and onion.
Enjoy!

Place the asparagus, oil and salt, if desired, in a bowl and toss gently to combine.

Heat a medium non-stick fry pan over medium-high heat and spray lightly with oil spray. Add the asparagus and cook for 3-5 minutes or until they start to change colour and are at desired
tenderness.
Toast the bread to your liking.
To serve, place the toast on a serving plate and top with the asparagus and eggs. Sprinkle over the parmesan.
Enjoy!

For the Curry

Start to prepare the rice according to the package instructions.
Chop the scallion and mince the garlic & ginger, set aside. Break the cauliflower into smaller florets and roughly chop the bell pepper, set aside.
To a medium hot pan, add the scallion, garlic, ginger and sauté on low-medium (or on medium if using oil) until the scallion start to become golden.
Add water to deglaze the pan plus all the spices (curry powder, curry paste, paprika powder, salt), add more splashes of water and stir the spices into a paste.
Now add the water, coconut milk and bring to a simmer. Then add the lentils, cauliflower, bell pepper.

Roughly cut the onion in big chunks. Peel the garlic.

Pour the soup into bowls, decorate with vegan unsweetened yogurt, bread cubes and some black pepper or chili flakes!

recipes.name: Pita triangles with Capsicum & White Bean Dip

instructions.text: Method
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.

Lay the pita wedges in a single layer on the lined baking tray and spray lightly with oil
spray. Bake in the oven for 5 minutes or until they begin to colour. Turn the wedges over and
bake for a further 5-8 minutes or until both sides are lightly coloured and set aside to cool.
To save time, the pita crisps can be cooked the night before and stored in an airtight container.

Meanwhile, place the capsicum skin side up under a hot grill and cook for 5-6 minutes or
until the skin has blackened. Transfer the capsicum to a small bowl, cover with plastic film
and set aside to cool. When cool enough to handle, peel off the blackened skin.

To make the dip, place the capsicum, cannellini beans and garlic in a food processor and pulse until smooth. Season with salt and pepper, if desired.
To save time, the dip can be made the night before and stored in an airtight container in the
refrigerator.
Serve the pita triangles with the capsicum and bean dip.
Enjoy!
Method

Heat a small non-stick frypan over medium- high heat and spray lightly with oil spray. Add
the tomato halves and cook for 5 minutes or until soft, stirring occasionally. Remove from the
pan and set aside on a plate.

Wipe the frypan clean and reheat over medium-high heat. Add the spinach and cook
for 1-2 minutes or until it begins to wilt. Remove from the pan and set aside on the plate with
the tomatoes.

To make the scrambled eggs, whisk the eggs, water, salt and pepper together in a bowl.
Reheat the frypan over medium heat. When the pan is hot, pour in the egg mixture. As the egg mixture begins to set, gently push it across the pan with a wooden spoon to form large folds.
Ensure that you push the mixture from different directions and include the mixture from around the edge of the pan. Do not stir constantly. Continue to cook until no visible liquid egg
remains and immediately remove from the heat.
Meanwhile, toast the bread to your liking.

To serve, place the toast on a serving plate and top with the spinach, tomatoes and
scrambled egg.
Enjoy!
1 batch = 4 slices

On a parchment paper, form the dough into a rectangle by using your hands. Then let it sit in the freezer while melting the chocolate over a water bath.
Carefully drizzle the melted chocolate over the dough. Use a spatula to even it out. Then place it in the fridge until the chocolate is set.
Now cut it into 4 equal bars (or whatever vou like) and store them in a container placed in the fridge or freezer!

Add the noodles to a bowl add the soup and tofu

Chop the coriander, chili and onion.

Enjoy!

Method
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).

Heat a medium saucepan over medium heat and spray lightly with oil spray. Add the onion, garlic and capsicum and cook for 2-3 minutes or until the onion is soft and translucent and capsicum is tender.

Add the diced sweet potato, black beans, half of the crushed tomatoes, coriander, cumin and chilli powder and stir to combine. Season with salt and pepper, if desired. If the mixture looks too thick, add a small splash of water.
Cover the saucepan with a lid and cook over a medium-low heat for 15-20 minutes or until the sweet potato is tender, stirring occasionally.

Bake for about 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese has melted and filling is hot.

To serve, place the enchilada on a serving plate and cut in half. Serve with the spinach on the
side.
Enioy!

Place the avocado in a small bowl and roughly mash using a fork. Add the onion, lemon
juice, salt and pepper and mix until well combined.

To poach the eggs, fill a saucepan with water until 8cm deep. Add the vinegar and bring to
the boil over a medium heat, then reduce the heat to medium-Iow. Break the eggs into the

To serve, place the toast on a serving plate and spread over the goat's cheese. Top with

recipes.name: Pasta Salad with Baked Tomatoes & Greens

instructions.text: Method
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Fill a large saucepan with water, add a pinch of salt and bring to the boil. Add the pasta and cook until al dente (see the pasta packet for recommended cooking time).

Place the tomatoes halves cut side up on the baking tray. Spray lightly with oil spray and
sprinkle over the dried herbs. Season with salt and pepper, if desired.

Bake in the oven for 10-15 minutes or until soft. Set aside to cool.
To save time, the tomatoes can be roasted the night before and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

To serve, place the pasta, tomatoes, spinach leaves, rocket leaves and butter beans in a
large bowl and toss gently to combine. Sprinkle over the shaved parmesan.
Enjoy!

recipes.name: Mushrooms & Tomato Frittata with Garden Salad

instructions.text: Ingredients
Method
Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced) and lightly spray a ramekin with oil spray.
Place the mushrooms and tomatoes in the prepared ramekin.
Whisk the eggs and milk together in a small bowl. Add the parsley and stir until well combined. Season with salt and pepper, if desired. Pour the egg mixture into the ramekin.

Bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes or until lightly golden and set. Remove from the oven and set aside to cool slightly. To save time, the frittata can be made the night before and stored in the refrigerator overnight.
To make the dressing, place the avocado, yoghurt and lemon juice in a high-powered blender and blend until smooth.

Place the lettuce, cucumber, carrot, snow peas and spring onion in a bowl. Drizzle over the dressing and toss gently to combine. To serve, place the frittata on a serving plate with the salad on the side.
Enjoy!
Ingredients
For the
Steps
In a pot over medium heat, add the onion, carrot, celery and garlic. Sauté until softened, approximately 5 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth, drained and rinsed can of white beans, lemon juice and lemon zest to the pot. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 10 minutes covered.
Stir in the kale, fresh dill and orzo and cook for another 10 minutes until the orzo is cooked through, keeping the lid off and stirring intermittently to prevent the orzo from sticking.
Stir in the tahini and turmeric until combined and heated through. Enjoy!
Ingredients
For the pancake
For the filling
Steps
First prepare the veggies. Slice the onion, mushroom and cherry tomatoes and set aside.
Add all chickpea pancake ingredients to a blender and mix until smooth.

In a non-stick pan on medium head, add a splash of vegetable oil and then add half of the batter (if
you're making for 2 servings) to the pan. Tilt the pan on all directions so that the batter is evenly round.

When the pancake starts to be bubbly and somewhat dry on the surface. flip it over and cook for
another minute. Set aside.
In the same pan sauté onion, soinach, tomatoes and mushrooms until onion is translucent.
Now fill the pancake on one side and then fold the other half over and eat immediately!
Method

Preheat the oven to 170C/345F (convection 160C/320F).
First blend the bananas and maple syrup in a blender until smooth (or throughly mash them in a bowl) it should be equal to a heaped 3/4 cup mashed banana not less.
In another bowl, add all dry ingredients and stir to combine.
Add in the banana liquid and stir to combine into a thick batter.
Add the batter to a small oven safe form covered in parchment paper I used a 12x18cm/47x7inches
Slice a banana into coins and place on top of the bread. Then bake for 65-70 minutes or until a stick comes out dry.
let the bread rest and cool down for at least 15 minutes before enjoying. Serve as it is or with some coconut vogurt and berries or why not some peanut butterl
Method
Place the cottage cheese and chives in a small bowl and mix until well combined.

To serve, spread the cottage cheese mix over the crispbreads and top with the cucumber and
capsicum.
Enjoy!
Method
Place all the ingredients in a high-powered blender and blend until smooth.
To serve, pour into a glass or jar and enjoy!
1 set = 16 bars
Ingredients
For the Bars
Steps
Preheat oven to 350° f and layer a large flat, rimmed baking tray with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine oats, coconut, pecans, coconut oil, cinnamon and nutmeg.
Mix well to combine. Place mixture in a single layer on the baking tray.
Bake for approximately 10 minutes until golden brown.
In a saucepan over medium heat, combine water, honey, dates and vanilla.
Simmer on low for 2-4 minutes until the dates are soft.
Place this mixture in a food processor, or blender and process until it’s a smooth paste.
In a large bowl, add the roasted oats mixture, the date paste and the chopped dried apples. Stir until well combined.
Press the mixture into a 13×9 pan, and cover. Refrigerate for 2 hours or until firm.
Remove from refrigerator and cut into 20 bars.
Enjoy!
Method
Place the oats, milk, yoghurt, pecans and cinnamon in a bowl and mix until well combined.

Pour the oat mixture into a bowl or jar. Cover with plastic film and place in the refrigerator to
chill overnight.
To serve, stir the mashed banana through the overnight oats.
Enjoy!

recipes.name: Pizza with Roasted Tomatoes, Mushrooms & Rocket

instructions.text: Method
Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced) and line a tray with baking paper.
Place the lentils and crushed tomatoes in a bowl and mix until well combined.
Lay the pita bread on a clean work surface and spread over the lentil and tomato mixture. Scatter over the mushrooms and tomato. Sprinkle over the cheese and season with salt and pepper, if desired.
Place the pizza on the lined tray and bake for 8-10 minutes or until the toppings are hot, the cheese has melted and the edges are golden brown.
To serve, place the pizza on a serving plate and top with the rocket.
Enjoy!

Method

To hard boil the egg, place the egg in a small saucepan and fill with cold water until it is covered by 2cm. Bring the water to the boil over a high heat. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 5-6 minutes.
To make the hummus, place the chickpeas, water, garlic, lemon juice, paprika and salt in a food processor and pulse until creamy.
To serve, slice the roll in half. On the bottom half of the roll, spread over the hummus and layer the spinach, cucumber, onion, beetroot and sliced egg. Top with other half of the roll.
Enjoy!

Fill a large saucepan with water, add a pinch of salt and bring to the boil. Add the pasta and cook until al dente (see the pasta packet for recommended cooking time). Drain and refresh under cool running water. Set aside to cool.
Place the beans, tomatoes, olives, onion, rocket, basil, chilli (if using) and pasta in a mixing bowl. Season with salt and pepper, if desired, and toss gently to combine.
To serve, place the pasta salad in a serving bowl.

Method

To make the hummus, place the chickpeas, water, garlic, lemon juice, paprika and salt in a food processor and pulse until creamy.
To save time, the hummus can be made the night before and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

To serve, place the wrap on a serving plate and spread over the hummus. Place the four bean mix, carrot, tomato, bean sprouts and lettuce down the middle of the wrap. Fold over the end and roll up to enclose the filling.
Enjoy!

Method

To make the tzatziki, peel and remove the seeds from the cucumber. Grate and squeeze
out any excess water. Place the yoghurt, cucumber, lemon juice, garlic, dill, salt and
pepper in a small bowl and mix until well combined.

Soak 2 wooden skewers in cold water for 30 minutes. This will stop the skewers from burning
when cooking the kebabs.
Preheat a barbecue or chargrill pan over high heat.

Place the tomatoes, onion, capsicum and zucchini in a large bowl. Drizzle over 1 1/4 tsp oil
and sprinkle over the oregano and paprika. Toss gently to combine.
Thread the tomatoes, onion, capsicum, zucchini and haloumi onto the skewers, alternating as you go. Grill for 5-8 minutes or until cooked, turning frequently.

In a small saucepan, bring the remaining olive oil and 180ml water to the boil. Add the
couscous and remove from the heat. Leave to stand, covered, for 2-3 minutes before "fluffing" with a fork to help separate the grains. Add the chickpeas, parsley and mint and stir until well combined.
To serve, place the couscous on a serving plate and top with the vegetable and haloumi skewers. Drizzle over the tzatziki.
Enjoy!
Method
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Place the pumpkin, capsicum, fennel, oil, garlic, salt and pepper, if desired, in a mixing bowl andtoss gently to combine. Ensure that all of the vegetables are lightly coated in the oil.
Place the vegetables onto the lined baking tray and roast in the oven for 10 minutes, then turn overand roast for a further 10 minutes until tender and cooked through.

To cook the quinoa, bring the quinoa and water to the boil in a small saucepan over high heat,
stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10-12 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the quinoa is tender.

To make the dressing, place the yoghurt, feta and lemon juice in a small bowl and stir well
to combine.

Place the roasted vegetables, quinoa, lentils and parsley in a mixing bowl and toss gently
to combine.
To serve, place the salad in a serving bowl and drizzle over the dressing.
Enjoy!
